videos.title: Cooper Expects $1,495 Average for Gold Price in 2011
videos.text: we're going to be talking about gold now mentioned it earlier it just uh it just hit a high yesterday trading off just a little bit um but for more on where gold goes from here we now go to Suki Cooper she's a precious metals Analyst at Barkley's Capital she's also a Bloomberg best forecaster of gold prices she likes gold in the short term but predicts we will see a turning point in the months to come well Suki joins us now from her firm in New York so good to have you Suki thanks for joining us on this Friday um let's talk a little bit about where gold is trading today we're off the high that was hit yesterday um but what is your what's your forecast we still expect gold prices to gain upward momentum so break the barriers that it's facing at the moment and hit new highs this year but we do think prices will Peak towards the end of this year and then start to ease what we saw at the start of the year was a lot of positive macro data was being priced in and that weighed upon prices we saw heavy profit taken but then the very strong Asian physical demand buying actually limited the downside but since then we've seen a number of issues propelling gold prices to Fresh all-time highs and we think prices are going to average 14.95 this year so we're still expecting fresh highs to be hit later on this year wow so you expect an average of 1495 for the Year talk to me about um clearly gold is the flight to safety trade um and we've we've certainly been seeing that with events in the Middle East North Africa and of course in Japan but let's talk about physical demand and then pure speculation how do you um when you're looking at the figures and trading activity how do you know which of those three strong physical demand flight to safety and speculation is driving the market what we tend to look at is premiums particularly physical premiums across various regions to give us a good idea of what the physical uh underlying demand the market is and also the trade data as well and the remarkable strength that we saw at the started this year really came from Asia but the interesting thing was that it wasn't centered in just one country we saw bar premiums H hitting multi-year highs in China in India in Tokyo in Singapore and in Hong Kong so there was a lot of underlying strength in the physical Market that limited the downside in gold prices but to get an idea of where we are in terms of investment demand we can track the ETF flows which give us a good idea of what longer term investors are thinking and the spective interest in terms of what the weekly comx non-commercial positions or where they are trading at the moment so what are um Suki what are the ETF flows indicating at the moment and um do you expect to see a turning point this year we they kicked off on a very weak note we saw the largest monthly outflow in January of 64 tons that weakness continued into February we saw another significant outflow but what we've seen is a turning point in March flows have actually stabilized and turned positive so even though we've seen net redemptions materializing across the ETFs the overall Holdings are still very strong and I think that's a sign that the underlying Market uncertainties are still encouraging investors to retain that long position in gold right now let me ask you in terms of macroeconomic factors that uh may be affecting trading in that flight to safety trade should we see things um calm in the Middle East or let me ask you what would be a sign from the Middle East and North Africa that things are calming down that might impact the trading on gold what we've seen in gold is not necessarily a direct correlation with the heightening of geopolitical tensions or the events in Japan but investors tend to allocate to gold on a more on a slower basis when we've had the Ono ongoing Market uncertainty so in the recent sessions where we've seen the European sovereign debt issues resurfacing we've continued to see investor interest or the longer term investor interest turning favorable towards gold so I think right now the ETF flows are absolutely essential to watch if the longer term investors are more comfortable with the macro Outlook then we'll see redemp and that will be a clear sign that the gold rally has run its course but at the moment there are so many uncertainties and on top of that interest rates are still very low making it a very attractive opportunity and a very attractive moment to hold gold so which um if you had to narrow down the most significant events as it relates to your forecast for gold what are they I'd say the physical flows are absolutely key because that's what's going to limit the downside for gold prices so if the the physical demand that we've seen in Asia starts to slow and that's a sign that the flaw that gold prices have found in recent sessions is a lot lower than where we've seen it at the start of the year I'd say the ETF flows are absolutely essential as well because it's been the longer term desire to hold gold as a portfolio diversifier as given its low correlation with other assets and as it's as a hedge against inflation long-term inflation concerns that's really made the story in Gold a long-term rally rather than just a spike so if those issues start to uh Wan we would expect gold prices start to ease as well and then Suki you do cover other metals as silver Palladium Platinum do any of those other metals have that sort of flight to safety quality we've seen sometimes the pgms benefiting a little bit but across the complex aside from gold it would be silver that piggybacks on Gold's Safe Haven buying properties and we've seen Silver really outperforming gold when the retail interest is picked up and it's really been a case of where the retail and communities feel like they've missed the boat in gold or gold is trading out of their price range that we've seen Silver benefit so that's something that's really come to light over the last 8 to 10 months where we've seen the investment demand in silver being propelled to incredibly high levels let me ask you this today both gold and silver are trading off a little bit do you think that's a trend that will continue at least in the short term is suggesting maybe that the rally was overdone very recently at the moment I'd say gold prices are struggling to break new high but I think it's still very impressive that even when we saw prices dipping towards 1400 and the high 1300s that the physical demand in Asia materialized we've actually seen Safe Haven buying coming across in Japan as well in in Western Japan where bar premiums there have hit threeyear highs so at the moment the physical demand even though it's not in a seasonly strong period has provided some support but I would say that we could see prices softening a little bit before they regain their upward momentum but your average for the year again just to clarify the forecast is 1490 1495 that's right 1495 now trading about I think 1426 when I last look Suki thanks so much for joining us

videos.title: Gold Prices | Gold Bullion | Gold Trading | Investment | 2011 Year In Review
videos.text: paul thanks so much for joining me in the studio glad to have you here instead of a little out of the shop i like it now you've brought some gold we've talked about gold prices so many times this year we're wrapping up 2011 can you can you give me just kind of a review a year in review and gold prices thank you for having us again kristen uh gold has had a wild ride this year we started the year at around 1400 an ounce it went up to as much as nineteen hundred dollars an ounce it's been up and down and we're going to wrap up the year somewhere in the 15 16 15 75 range that'll be a 10 to 12 increase for the for gold so that's good it's better than most conventional portfolios are doing and um and of course if you had the wisdom to get out in the 1900 range you had to get a great choice yeah those were record highs right that we saw this world record highs at 1900. unreal now what about right now where where is the state of the market right now gold is in the 15 16 15 70 range now keep in mind in the end of december there are a lot of the fund managers that are wrapping up their portfolios and and gold is a hedge so it's time to liquidate those assets and turn them back into money and say this is what we performed this is our performance for the year end so it's expected that gold drops at the end of december and then right through january we get a little bit of pickup but in february we really get that push again okay so yeah give me a little bit of a forecast of 2012. so we're looking at a drop-off coming here pretty soon and then what's going to happen from there 2012 all conventional wisdom is that we're going to have a soft january in medals uh as we ride the end of january early february we're going to have a pickup in the metals and a lot depends upon the global politics of course euro the euro is is somewhat stabilizing the the economy there is getting better at least temporarily but the long-term prognosis is not good for the euro or for the dollar now if you were one of those people who held out through those 1900 record highs and you still had some gold right now what would you recommend getting rid of it right now or of course cashing it in right now or holding out a little longer no it's a it's clearly a hole you know if you're in the medals it's not a short-term game it's a long-term game for for example if you're a take a look at the last five years okay if you're a five-year holder of gold gold with five years ago was six hundred dollars an ounce and now it's fifteen hundred dollars that's triple well nothing has tripled particularly in a downturn economy so it's a great long-term play do you think potentially we could see those kind of record highs again in 2012 2000. wow yeah so it could be a good like you said it's it's a it's a long-term market and of course you want to check it out uh check paul out of course at manor auctions or grace estate buyers your expert opinion and we're going to have your opinion tomorrow on the silver market market a wrap up and of course a preview of 2012 there too right thank you chris thank you so much

videos.title: Gold Prices Up
videos.text: people are rushing to invest in gold prices up as never seen before and here's ABC's John Burman the partisanship politicking and lack of progress in Washington might already be taking a toll on your personal bank account in 401K the stock dropped today the first sign investors are saying we're mad as hell and we're not going to take it anymore the impatience and the frustration is clearly getting there Wall Street has a way of voicing its displeasure and its downright other L remember September 2008 when Congress failed to pass a bank bailout stocks traded nearly 780 points that day 3.5 million for sale PA on a million we're not there yet but there are other unnerving signs call it a canary in a gold mine gold prices are skyrocketing closing above 1,600 for the first time ever 500% higher than 10 years ago why is this troubling it's investors betting there will be bedum no solution to the debt mess fearing a market meltdown gold is seen as a safe haven the same concept of taking the money out of the market and putting under your mattress investors are uh taking money out of the market and putting it straight into gold Those ads on TV explain the new age Gold Rush listen to that that's the sound of security earlier this year the University of Texas even put $1 billion of their endowment into gold literally storing more than 6,000 gold bars in a vault and these prices are one of the Key signs to watch the next two weeks if gold prices continue this day after day after day increase it means investors really are betting on something close to a meltdown and also us treasuries they're usually considered one of the safest Investments but if investors stop buying them it means they feel there really isn't much left that's safe so these are the early Warning Systems the canaries and the gold mine as I call them thanks so much John
